61	  "
62	
63	  local z_field=""
64	  for z_field in ${z_compose_fields}; do
65	    local z_value="${!z_field:-}"
66	    # Check for quotes (compose does not strip them)
67	    case "${z_value}" in
68	      *\"*|*\'*)
69	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains quotes — compose --env-file does not strip quotes, they become part of the value"
70	        ;;
71	    esac
72	    # Check for ${VAR} references (compose interpolates them)
73	    case "${z_value}" in
74	      *'${'*)
75	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains \${VAR} reference — compose --env-file interpolates these, causing unintended expansion"
76	        ;;
77	    esac
78	  done
79	}
80	
81	######################################################################
82	# Kindle and Sentinel
83	
84	zrbob_kindle() {
85	  test -z "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob already kindled"
86	
87	  # Verify RBRN regime is kindled (provides nameplate config)
88	  zrbrn_sentinel
89	
90	  # Validate compose-consumed fields before proceeding
91	  zrbob_validate_compose_env
92	
93	  # Verify RBRR regime is kindled (provides repo config like DNS_SERVER)
94	  zrbrr_sentinel
95	
96	  # Runtime command (docker or podman)
97	  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
98	    docker) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="docker" ;;
99	    podman) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="podman" ;;
100	    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRN_RUNTIME: ${RBRN_RUNTIME}" ;;
101	  esac
102	
103	  # Container names (for connect and info commands)
104	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY="${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry"
105	  readonly ZRBOB_PENTACLE="${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle"
106	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE="${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle"
107	
108	  # Network name (compose names as {project}_{network}; used by observe and info)
109	  readonly ZRBOB_NETWORK="${RBRN_MONIKER}_enclave"
110	
111	  # Compose file paths (relative to project root, where compose runs)
112	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbob_compose.yml"
113	  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
114	
115	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/compose.yml"
116	  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time
117	
118	  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
119	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbrr.env"
120	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
121	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
122	
123	
124	  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
125	  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
126	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
127	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
128	
129	  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
130	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"
131	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"
132	
133	  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
134	  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
135	  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
136	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
137	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
138	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
139	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
140	  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
141	  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
142	  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
143	  export RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
144	  export RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
145	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
146	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
147	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
148	  export RBRR_DNS_SERVER
149	  export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE
150	
151	  readonly ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_drive_"
152	
153	  readonly ZRBOB_KINDLED=1
154	}
155	
156	zrbob_sentinel() {
157	  test "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbob not kindled - call zrbob_kindle first"
158	}

RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
  "

  local z_field=""
  for z_field in ${z_compose_fields}; do
    local z_value="${!z_field:-}"
    # Check for quotes (compose does not strip them)
    case "${z_value}" in
      *\"*|*\'*)
        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains quotes — compose --env-file does not strip quotes, they become part of the value"
        ;;
    esac
    # Check for ${VAR} references (compose interpolates them)
    case "${z_value}" in
      *'${'*)
        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains \${VAR} reference — compose --env-file interpolates these, causing unintended expansion"
        ;;
    esac
  done
}

######################################################################
# Kindle and Sentinel

zrbob_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob already kindled"

  # Verify RBRN regime is kindled (provides nameplate config)
  zrbrn_sentinel

  # Validate compose-consumed fields before proceeding
  zrbob_validate_compose_env

  # Verify RBRR regime is kindled (provides repo config like DNS_SERVER)
  zrbrr_sentinel

  # Runtime command (docker or podman)
  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
    docker) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="docker" ;;
    podman) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="podman" ;;
    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRN_RUNTIME: ${RBRN_RUNTIME}" ;;
  esac

  # Container names (for connect and info commands)
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY="${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry"
  readonly ZRBOB_PENTACLE="${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE="${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle"

  # Network name (compose names as {project}_{network}; used by observe and info)
  readonly ZRBOB_NETWORK="${RBRN_MONIKER}_enclave"

  # Compose file paths (relative to project root, where compose runs)
  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbob_compose.yml"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"

  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/compose.yml"
  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time

  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbrr.env"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"


  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"

  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"

  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
  export RBRR_DNS_SERVER
  export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE

  readonly ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_drive_"

  readonly ZRBOB_KINDLED=1
}

zrbob_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbob not kindled - call zrbob_kindle first"
}

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBOB CLI - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle command-line interface
20	#
21	# Commands:
22	#   start           Start crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle)
23	#   stop            Stop crucible
24	#   hail            Hail sentry (interactive shell)
25	#   rack            Rack bottle (interactive shell)
26	#   scry            Scry network traffic (tcpdump)
27	#   info            Show container names, network, and runtime
28	#   validate        Validate configuration is complete
29	
30	set -euo pipefail
31	
32	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
33	
34	######################################################################
35	# CLI Commands
36	
37	rbob_validate() {
38	  zrbob_sentinel
39	
40	  buc_doc_brief "Validate that RBOB configuration is complete and runnable"
41	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
42	
43	  buc_step "RBOB Validate: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
44	
45	  # All values computed at kindle - just verify they exist
46	  test -n "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_RUNTIME not set"
47	  test -n "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_SENTRY not set"
48	  test -n "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_PENTACLE not set"
49	  test -n "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_BOTTLE not set"
50	  test -n "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_NETWORK not set"
51	  test -f "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_SCRIPT}" || buc_die "Sentry script not found: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_SCRIPT}"
52	  test -f "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE_SCRIPT}" || buc_die "Pentacle script not found: ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE_SCRIPT}"
53	  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Compose base not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
54	
55	  buc_step "RBOB configuration valid"
56	  echo "Moniker:       ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
57	  echo "Runtime:       ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}"
58	  echo "Sentry:        ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
59	  echo "Pentacle:        ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
60	  echo "Bottle:        ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
61	  echo "Network:       ${ZRBOB_NETWORK}"
62	  echo "Compose base:  ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"

103	# Furnish and Main

140	    buc_step "Available nameplates:"

143	      buc_bare "        ${z_moniker}"
144	    done
145	    buc_die "Nameplate moniker required (pass as argument)"
146	  fi
147	  local z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_folio}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
148	  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate not found: ${z_nameplate_file}"
149	  source "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_nameplate_file}"
150	  zrbrn_kindle

153	  zrbrr_kindle

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBOB CLI - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle command-line interface
#
# Commands:
#   start           Start crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle)
#   stop            Stop crucible
#   hail            Hail sentry (interactive shell)
#   rack            Rack bottle (interactive shell)
#   scry            Scry network traffic (tcpdump)
#   info            Show container names, network, and runtime
#   validate        Validate configuration is complete

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# CLI Commands

rbob_validate() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Validate that RBOB configuration is complete and runnable"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "RBOB Validate: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  # All values computed at kindle - just verify they exist
  test -n "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_RUNTIME not set"
  test -n "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_SENTRY not set"
  test -n "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_PENTACLE not set"
  test -n "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_BOTTLE not set"
  test -n "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || buc_die "ZRBOB_NETWORK not set"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_SCRIPT}" || buc_die "Sentry script not found: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_SCRIPT}"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE_SCRIPT}" || buc_die "Pentacle script not found: ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE_SCRIPT}"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Compose base not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"

  buc_step "RBOB configuration valid"
  echo "Moniker:       ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
  echo "Runtime:       ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}"
  echo "Sentry:        ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  echo "Pentacle:        ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
  echo "Bottle:        ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  echo "Network:       ${ZRBOB_NETWORK}"
  echo "Compose base:  ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
  echo "Compose frag:  ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT} ($(test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}" && echo 'exists' || echo 'not found'))"
}

rbob_info() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Show container names, network, and runtime for kindled nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "RBOB Info: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
  echo "Runtime:   ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}"
  echo "Sentry:    ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  echo "Pentacle:    ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
  echo "Bottle:    ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  echo "Network:   ${ZRBOB_NETWORK}"
  echo "Sentry IP: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"
  echo "Bottle IP: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
}

rbob_scry() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Scry network traffic on crucible containers"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Kindle observe module and delegate
  zrboo_kindle
  rboo_observe
}

rbob_charged() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  rbob_charged_predicate
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrbob_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BUZ_FOLIO             " "Nameplate moniker (e.g., tadmor)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  local z_command="${1:-}"

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrn_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbob_bottle.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rboo_observe.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zrbcc_kindle

  local z_folio="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  if test -z "${z_folio}"; then
    local z_monikers
    z_monikers=$(rbrn_list_capture) || buc_die "No nameplates found"
    buc_step "Available nameplates:"
    local z_moniker=""
    for z_moniker in ${z_monikers}; do
      buc_bare "        ${z_moniker}"
    done
    buc_die "Nameplate moniker required (pass as argument)"
  fi
  local z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_folio}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate not found: ${z_nameplate_file}"
  source "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_nameplate_file}"
  zrbrn_kindle
  zrbrn_enforce

  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbdc_kindle
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgd_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbob_kindle

  # Differential kindle: foundry modules for kludge/ordain commands
  case "${z_command}" in
    rbob_kludge|rbob_kludge_bottle|rbob_ordain)
      zrbfd_kindle
      ;;
  esac

  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rbob_ "Recipe Bottle Orchestration" zrbob_furnish "$@"

160	######################################################################
161	# Compose Command Helper
162	
163	# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
164	# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
165	zrbob_compose() {
166	  zrbob_sentinel
167	
168	  local z_args=()
169	  z_args+=("compose")
170	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}")
171	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}")
172	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}")
173	  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}")
174	
175	  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists
176	  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
177	    z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
178	  fi
179	
180	  z_args+=("-p" "${RBRN_MONIKER}")
181	  z_args+=("$@")
182	
183	  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
184	  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
185	}
186	
187	######################################################################
188	# Auto-Summon Helper
189	
190	# Verify vouch exists and pull image if missing locally
191	# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
192	zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
193	  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
194	  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
195	  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"
196	
197	  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
198	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
199	  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"
200	
201	  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
202	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
203	
204	  local z_token
205	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"
206	
207	  # Pull the image
208	  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"
209	
210	  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
211	    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"
212	
213	  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"
214	
215	  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
216	}
217	
218	######################################################################
219	# Subnet Conflict Detection
220	
221	# Die if another Docker network already occupies this nameplate's subnet.
222	# This catches stale networks from prior compose projects that died without
223	# compose-down (reboot, Docker Desktop restart, crash). Our own network
224	# (from the compose-down above) is excluded.
225	zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict() {
226	  zrbob_sentinel
227	
228	  local z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
229	  local z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_networks.txt"
230	  local z_subnet_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_subnet.txt"
231	
232	  # List all Docker network names
233	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' > "${z_networks_file}" \
234	    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks"
235	
236	  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG)
237	  local z_names=()
238	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
239	    z_names+=("${z_line}")
240	  done < "${z_networks_file}"
241	
242	  # Inspect each network's subnet
243	  local z_name=""
244	  for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
245	    # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
246	    test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue
247	
248	    ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
249	      --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
250	      > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>/dev/null || continue
251	
252	    local z_subnet=""
253	    z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")
254	
255	    if test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}"; then
256	      buc_die "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} already claimed by stale network '${z_name}' — clean up manually: docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_name}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_name}"
257	    fi
258	  done
259	}
260	
261	######################################################################
262	# Public API
263	
264	# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
265	# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
266	rbob_charge() {
267	  zrbob_sentinel
268	
269	  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
270	
271	  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
272	  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
273	    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
274	  fi
275	
276	  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
277	  rbrn_preflight
278	
279	  # Preflight: verify vouch artifacts exist locally — no network calls, fatal if missing
280	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
281	    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
282	    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
283	    buc_die "Run summon to pull vouch artifact before starting"
284	  fi
285	
286	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
287	    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
288	    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
289	    buc_die "Run summon to pull vouch artifact before starting"
290	  fi
291	
292	  # Preflight: verify container images exist locally, auto-summon if missing
293	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
294	    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
295	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
296	  fi
297	
298	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
299	    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
300	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
301	  fi
302	
303	  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
304	  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state"
305	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
306	
307	  # Defensive check: another compose project may hold a network on our subnet
308	  # (e.g., nsproto containers died without compose-down, blocking tadmor's subnet)
309	  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict
310	
311	  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
312	  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
313	  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
314	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait
315	
316	  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
317	}
318	
319	# Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers).
320	# BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not. Never dies, no output.
321	rbob_charged_predicate() {
322	  zrbob_sentinel
323	  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" compose -p "${RBRN_MONIKER}" ps -q --status running 2>/dev/null | grep -q .
324	}
325	
326	# Stop the crucible via compose
327	rbob_quench() {
328	  zrbob_sentinel
329	
330	  buc_step "Stopping crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
331	
332	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans
333	
334	  buc_step "Crucible stopped: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
335	}
336	
337	# Hail sentry — call out to the guard (interactive shell)
338	rbob_hail() {
339	  zrbob_sentinel
340	  buc_step "Hailing sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
341	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" /bin/bash
342	}
343	
344	# SSH into bottle — proper terminal session via sentry DNAT
345	rbob_ssh() {
346	  zrbob_sentinel
347	  buc_step "SSH to bottle via port ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
348	  exec ssh -t -p "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
349	    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null \
350	    claude@localhost
351	}
352	
353	# Rack bottle — compel the demon to reveal state (interactive shell)
354	rbob_rack() {
355	  zrbob_sentinel
356	  buc_step "Racking bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
357	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" /bin/bash
358	}
359

######################################################################
# Compose Command Helper

# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
zrbob_compose() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local z_args=()
  z_args+=("compose")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}")
  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}")

  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists
  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
    z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
  fi

  z_args+=("-p" "${RBRN_MONIKER}")
  z_args+=("$@")

  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
}

######################################################################
# Auto-Summon Helper

# Verify vouch exists and pull image if missing locally
# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"

  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"

  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"

  # Pull the image
  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"

  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"

  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"

  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
}

######################################################################
# Subnet Conflict Detection

# Die if another Docker network already occupies this nameplate's subnet.
# This catches stale networks from prior compose projects that died without
# compose-down (reboot, Docker Desktop restart, crash). Our own network
# (from the compose-down above) is excluded.
zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
  local z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_networks.txt"
  local z_subnet_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_subnet.txt"

  # List all Docker network names
  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' > "${z_networks_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks"

  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG)
  local z_names=()
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_names+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_networks_file}"

  # Inspect each network's subnet
  local z_name=""
  for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
    # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
    test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue

    ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
      --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
      > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>/dev/null || continue

    local z_subnet=""
    z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")

    if test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}"; then
      buc_die "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} already claimed by stale network '${z_name}' — clean up manually: docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_name}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_name}"
    fi
  done
}

######################################################################
# Public API

# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
rbob_charge() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
  fi

  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
  rbrn_preflight

  # Preflight: verify vouch artifacts exist locally — no network calls, fatal if missing
  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
    buc_die "Run summon to pull vouch artifact before starting"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
    buc_die "Run summon to pull vouch artifact before starting"
  fi

  # Preflight: verify container images exist locally, auto-summon if missing
  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
  fi

  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true

  # Defensive check: another compose project may hold a network on our subnet
  # (e.g., nsproto containers died without compose-down, blocking tadmor's subnet)
  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict

  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait

  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

# Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers).
# BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not. Never dies, no output.
rbob_charged_predicate() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" compose -p "${RBRN_MONIKER}" ps -q --status running 2>/dev/null | grep -q .
}

# Stop the crucible via compose
rbob_quench() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_step "Stopping crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans

  buc_step "Crucible stopped: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

# Hail sentry — call out to the guard (interactive shell)
rbob_hail() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Hailing sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" /bin/bash
}

# SSH into bottle — proper terminal session via sentry DNAT
rbob_ssh() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "SSH to bottle via port ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
  exec ssh -t -p "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null \
    claude@localhost
}

# Rack bottle — compel the demon to reveal state (interactive shell)
rbob_rack() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Racking bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" /bin/bash
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Regime Vessel - Validator Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBRV_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrv multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBRV_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbrv_*)

zrbrv_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBRV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrv already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all RBRV variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll RBRV

  buv_group_enroll "Core Vessel Identity"
  buv_xname_enroll  RBRV_SIGIL             1   64  "Unique identifier (must match directory name)"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_DESCRIPTION       0  512  "Human-readable description"
  buv_enum_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE               "Operation mode: bind, conjure, or graft" \
                    bind conjure graft

  buv_group_enroll "Tool Image Reliquary"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_RELIQUARY             1   14  "Reliquary datestamp (e.g., r260324193326) — identifies tool image set in GAR"

  buv_group_enroll "Egress Mode"
  buv_enum_enroll   RBRV_EGRESS_MODE                   "Pool routing for primary build operation" \
                    tether airgap

  buv_group_enroll "Binding Configuration"
  buv_gate_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE  bind
  buv_fqin_enroll   RBRV_BIND_IMAGE                1  512  "Source image to copy from registry"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_BIND_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE  0  512  "Optional Dockerfile for about recipe.txt"

  buv_group_enroll "Conjuring Configuration"
  buv_gate_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE  conjure
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE    1  512  "Dockerfile path relative to repo root"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT    1  512  "Build context relative to repo root"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS     1  512  "Space-separated target platforms"

  buv_group_enroll "Image Group"
  buv_gate_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE  conjure
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN   0  512  "Upstream base image tag slot 1 (e.g., python:3.11-slim)"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR   0  512  "GAR-mirrored anchor slot 1 (written by enshrine)"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN   0  512  "Upstream base image tag slot 2"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_2_ANCHOR   0  512  "GAR-mirrored anchor slot 2 (written by enshrine)"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN   0  512  "Upstream base image tag slot 3"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_IMAGE_3_ANCHOR   0  512  "GAR-mirrored anchor slot 3 (written by enshrine)"

  buv_group_enroll "Grafting Configuration"
  buv_gate_enroll   RBRV_VESSEL_MODE  graft
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE                1  512  "Local image reference for graft operations"
  buv_string_enroll RBRV_GRAFT_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE  0  512  "Optional Dockerfile for about recipe.txt"

  # Guard against unexpected RBRV_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel RBRV RBRV_

  # Lock all enrolled RBRV_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock RBRV

  readonly ZRBRV_KINDLED=1
}

zrbrv_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBRV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrv not kindled - call zrbrv_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all RBRV enrollment validations
zrbrv_enforce() {
  zrbrv_sentinel

  buv_vet RBRV
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (rbrv_*)

# List available vessel sigils as space-separated tokens
# Prerequisite: RBRR kindled (needs RBRR_VESSEL_DIR)
rbrv_list_capture() {
  zrbrr_sentinel

  local z_result=""
  local z_dirs=("${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"/*)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_dirs[@]}"; do
    local z_d="${z_dirs[$z_i]}"
    test -d "${z_d}" || continue
    test -f "${z_d}/rbrv.env" || continue
    local z_s="${z_d%/}"
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_s##*/}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

= Recipe Bottle Specification
:toc:
:toc-title: Table of Contents
:sectnums:
:icons: font
:index:

// Begin Mapping Section
// tag::mapping-section[]

// Category declarations:
// rbtr_ prefix: RB Term Role (Recipe Bottle role identities)
// rbtgi_ prefix: RB Term Google Instance (Recipe Bottle specific instances)
// rbtgo_ prefix: RB Google Operation (Recipe Bottle workflows)
// gcp_ prefix: Google Cloud Platform (core infrastructure concepts)
// gar_ prefix: Google Artifact Registry (generic service)
// gcs_ prefix: Google Cloud Storage (generic service)
// gcb_ prefix: Google Cloud Build (generic service)
// giam_ prefix: Google Cloud Identity and Access Management
// rbtc_ prefix: RB Term Colophon (tabtarget entry points)
// rbrr_ prefix: Repository Regime configuration

// Local Architecture Terms (bottle deployment architecture):
// at_ prefix: Architectural Term (core system components)
// st_ prefix: Support Term (infrastructure and technical concepts)

// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//   _ed: past tense (verbs)
//   _ing: progressive form (verbs)

// RB Role Infrastructure
:rbtr_role:                  <<rbtr_role,RB Role>>
:rbtr_role_s:                <<rbtr_role,RB Roles>>

// Service Account Hierarchy
:rbtr_consumer:              <<rbtr_consumer,Consumer Role>>
:rbtr_consumer_p:            <<rbtr_consumer,Consumer Role's>>
:rbtr_consumer_s:            <<rbtr_consumer,Consumer Roles>>
:rbtr_director:              <<rbtr_director,Director Role>>
:rbtr_director_s:            <<rbtr_director,Director Roles>>
:rbtr_governor:              <<rbtr_governor,Governor Role>>
:rbtr_marshal:               <<rbtr_marshal,Marshal Role>>
:rbtr_mason:                 <<rbtr_mason,Mason Role>>
:at_operator:                <<at_operator,Operator>>
:at_operator_p:              <<at_operator,Operator's>>
:at_operator_s:              <<at_operator,Operators>>
:rbtr_payor:                 <<rbtr_payor,Payor Role>>
:rbtr_payor_p:               <<rbtr_payor,Payor Role's>>
:rbtr_retriever:             <<rbtr_retriever,Retriever Role>>
:rbtr_retriever_s:           <<rbtr_retriever,Retriever Roles>>

// Recipe Bottle Term Google Instances
:rbtgi_build_bucket:         <<rbtgi_build_bucket,RB Build Bucket>>

== System Overview

=== Core Trust Challenge

Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
{st_container}-based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.

This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:

*
{st_container_s}
provide powerful tool isolation
* But they need controlled access to do useful work
* Standard
{st_container}
networking grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.

=== The Bottle Pattern

{at_rbm_system}
solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
{st_container}
type here referred to as a
{at_bottle_container}:

. File Access Boundary
*
{at_bottle_container}
sees only explicitly controlled set of
{st_volume_mount_s}
* No other access to
{at_workstation}
filesystem

. Network Boundary
*
{at_bottle_container}
has no direct network access
* All traffic flows through dedicated
{at_sentry_container},
a
{st_container}
configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
* Only declared
{st_port_map_s}
and destinations allowed
* Full logging of allowed and denied traffic

Each
{rbsc_crucible}
maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:

* One
{at_sentry_container}
enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
* One
{at_pentacle_container}
establishing and configuring the network namespace
* One
{at_bottle_container}
running the actual service, sharing the pentacle's network namespace via compose `network_mode: "service:pentacle"`
* Clear configuration in a single
{at_nameplate}
file, extended by per-nameplate compose fragments for volume mounts

Docker Compose orchestrates the container lifecycle declaratively.
A base compose file (`rbob_compose.yml`) defines the three-container topology with health check chains: the
{at_sentry_container}
must be healthy before the
{at_pentacle_container}
starts, and the
{at_pentacle_container}
must be healthy before the
{at_bottle_container}
starts.
This prevents firewall bypass — the bottle cannot send traffic before security policies are enforced.
Per-nameplate compose fragments (e.g., `«moniker».compose.yml`) add volume mount declarations.
Env-files passed via `--env-file` serve a dual purpose: YAML interpolation (`${VAR}` references in compose files resolve image refs, subnet, container names) and container environment forwarding (bare variable names in `environment:` sections inject values into containers at runtime).

The
{at_pentacle_container}
is a privileged container that configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
{at_sentry_container},
then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
{at_bottle_container}
via compose `network_mode: "service:pentacle"`.
This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
{at_bottle_container}
experiences only a functional path to its
{at_sentry_container}
gateway

=== Security Properties

The
{at_rbm_system}
maintains following essential guarantees:

. File Access Control
*
{at_bottle_container_s}
can access only explicitly configured
{st_volume_mount_s}
* No access to broader system resources

. Network Isolation
* All traffic flows through dedicated
{at_sentry_container}
* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
* Only explicitly configured
{st_port_map_s}
allow traffic between
{at_transit_network}
and
{at_enclave_network}
*
{at_bottle_container_s}
have no direct network access; all connectivity flows through
{at_sentry_container_s}
* Comprehensive traffic logging

. Service Separation
* Each
{rbsc_crucible}
fully isolated
* No cross-service communication
* Independent security policies
* Clean state separation

. Service Access Control
* External access to services only permitted when
{rbrn_entry_mode}
is enabled
* No inter-container communication allowed within pods
* All service access must originate from outside the pod
* Entry traffic flows: external -> pod ->
{at_sentry_container}
->
{at_bottle_container}

These properties hold across all operations and failure modes.

== Google API Engagement

{at_recipe_bottle}
invokes
{gcp_service}
APIs through
{giam_service_account}
JWT authentication and direct HTTPS REST calls.

* *Authentication*:
{giam_service_account_s}
use private key credentials to sign JWTs with RS256.
These JWTs are exchanged for
{oauth_token_s}
via Google's token endpoint.
All API calls include the bearer token in the Authorization header.

* *Invocation Pattern*:
Operations execute through curl with JSON payloads and responses.
Each call captures both HTTP status codes and response bodies to files for processing.
Success is determined by HTTP 200/201/204 codes.

* *Long-Running Operations*:
{gcp_service}
{gcp_lro_s}
return operation IDs that must be polled until completion.
The system polls at configurable intervals until completion.
The system handles both success and error states.
Operations utilize
{rbbc_submit}
and
{rbbc_await}
to handle all asynchronous operations.

* *IAM Policies*:
All IAM policy modifications enforce version 3 to ensure conditional role binding support across all
{gcp_service}
resources.

* *IAM Member Format*:
All IAM member bindings use `serviceAccount:«EMAIL»` format.
+
GCP IAM policy `members` fields only accept email-format identifiers (`serviceAccount:name@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com`).
The numeric `uniqueId` returned by `serviceAccounts.create` is valid for *API URL paths* (e.g., `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«UNIQUE_ID»` for GET/verification) but *not* for policy binding members.
`serviceAccount:«NUMERIC_ID»` returns HTTP 400 "Invalid service account" on all setIamPolicy endpoints — project, repository, and bucket alike.
+
Google's documentation recommends numeric IDs "to identify a service account just after it is created" (link:https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/manage-access-service-accounts[Manage access to service accounts]), but this applies to API resource addressing, not to IAM policy member strings.
The internal IAM system resolves email→numeric transparently; callers must use email.
+
Google-provisioned service agents (Cloud Build) are not affected — they are managed by Google, not newly created.

== Operational Invariants

=== Concurrent Session Safety

{at_recipe_bottle}
commands are designed for concurrent execution from multiple independent sessions against the same filesystem.
This enables parallel workflows where different developers or automation systems invoke commands simultaneously.

Concurrency safety is achieved through:

* *Ephemeral Isolation*:
Each command invocation creates a unique temporary directory using `BURD_NOW_STAMP` (timestamp + process ID + random component), ensuring intermediate files never collide.

* *Unique Artifact Naming*:
Cloud resources (build IDs, inscribe timestamps) incorporate unique identifiers, preventing overwrite conflicts in
{rbtgi_repository}.

* *Read-Only Git Queries*:
Git operations are non-mutating (status checks, metadata reads).
The `git fetch` operation uses git's internal locking for safe concurrent execution.

* *Semantic Preconditions*:
Operations like
{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
require clean git state.
This is a workflow constraint ensuring reproducible builds, not a collision guard.

NOTE: Concurrent builds of the same vessel produce independent images with different timestamps.
Both builds succeed; neither overwrites the other.

NOTE: The
{gcb_service}
private pool enforces a *Concurrent Build CPUs (Private Pool)* quota per project per region.
Machine type is set at pool creation time via
{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}
(a Compute Engine type such as `e2-standard-2`); individual build requests do not specify machine type.
If the pool's vCPU allocation is nearly exhausted, a second
build invocation will remain in `QUEUED` status while the first is `WORKING`.
The
{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
regime variable declares the operator's minimum concurrency requirement.
See
{rbtgo_quota_build}
for the manual Console procedure to review build capacity settings.

== Key Premises

[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
{at_recipe_bottle}
scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
All local
{gcp_service}
interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
+
**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.
This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
+
In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
+
Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
+
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because conjure build steps use it as a convenience shell (for `bash` and `date`).
The vouch verification step (conjure mode) additionally invokes `gcloud` for provenance retrieval (see exception above).

== Dependency Inventory

{at_recipe_bottle} depends on a minimal, explicitly managed set of external commands.
The Bash Console Guide (`BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md`) establishes the universal dependency discipline — POSIX builtins preferred, external commands justified individually.
This section inventories the concrete choices {at_recipe_bottle} has made within that framework.

=== Consumer Dependencies

Required on every {at_workstation}.
Enforced by qualification and documented on the project page.

[cols="1,3"]
|===
| Command | Purpose

| `bash`
| Shell runtime (3.2+ compatible)

| `git`
| Version control and subtree integration

| `curl`
| HTTP client for all GCP REST API interaction

| `jq`
| JSON parsing for API responses and configuration

| `docker`
| {at_runtime} for bottle lifecycle

| `openssl`
| JWT signing (RS256), base64 encoding/decoding, SHA-256 hashing

| `ssh` / `scp` / `ssh-keygen`
| Secure credential transport and key generation
|===

=== Developer Dependencies

Used in development and testing tooling.
Not required for consumer workflows.

[cols="1,3"]
|===
| Command | Purpose

| `shellcheck`
| Static analysis linter, used by release-tier qualification

| `timeout`
| GNU coreutils test timeout, used by testbench; absent on stock macOS
|===

=== Specialized Runtime Dependencies

Present in specific operational modules.
Not required for core consumer or developer workflows.

[cols="1,3"]
|===
| Command | Purpose

| `podman`
| Alternative container runtime (deferred: dual-runtime support alongside docker)

| `tcpdump`
| Network debugging in observe module

| `sed`
| 2 load-bearing uses: log timestamp normalization, Podman output parsing

| `stty`
| Terminal width detection in launcher
|===

{rbtga_ark_image}
by building a container image
({rbtgog_ark})
from a vessel specification.
Build context delivered via pouch (FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR), submitted via `builds.create` API. The combined job produces both `-image` and `-about` (about steps embedded via stitch). Vouch is a separate Cloud Build job using DSSE envelope signature verification.

include::RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_enshrine]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_enshrine}

Enshrines upstream base images into
{gar_service}
({rbtgog_ark}),
producing content-addressed
{rbrv_image_anchor}
references from
{rbrv_image_origin}
declarations.
The
{rbtr_director}
submits a Cloud Build job that copies each upstream image by tag to GAR with a human-readable anchor tag, and writes the anchor back to the vessel regime.

include::RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_graft]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_graft}

Grafts a locally-built container image into the
{rbtgi_repository}
({rbtgog_ark})
via native docker/podman push.
The image push itself is local (no Cloud Build); about and vouch follow as Cloud Build jobs.

include::RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_about]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_about}

Assembles the
{rbtga_ark_about}
metadata artifact for an existing
{rbtga_ark_image}
({rbtgog_ark}).
Runs Syft for SBOM generation and builds mode-aware `build_info.json` using uniform
{gcb_service}
steps.
Conjure and bind embed these steps in their primary Cloud Build job alongside image production; graft's primary job consists of these steps alone.

include::RBSAB-ark_about.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_vouch]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_vouch}

Vouches for an
{rbtga_ark}
({rbtgog_ark}),
producing a
{rbtga_ark_vouch}
that records mode-aware verification results.
Conjure: DSSE envelope signature verification (Python 3 + openssl against GCB attestor keys).
Bind: digest-pin comparison.
Graft: GRAFTED stamp.
Submitted as a
{gcb_service}
job via `builds.create` after
{rbtgo_ark_about}
completes.

include::RBSAV-ark_vouch.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_director_vouch]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_director_vouch}

Batch-vouches all pending
{rbtga_ark}
hallmarks across all
{rbtga_vessel}
repositories
({rbtgog_ark}).
Bind and graft hallmarks are always vouchable; conjure hallmarks require SLSA provenance pre-check.

include::RBSDV-director_vouch.adoc[]

== Term Definitions

=== Core {gcp_project_id} Terms

[[at_recipe_bottle]]
{at_recipe_bottle}::
The comprehensive container management platform that enables secure, role-based building, deployment, and retrieval of containerized applications through
{gcp_service}
infrastructure.

=== RB Role Infrastructure

[[rbtr_role]]
//axl_voices axo_identity
{rbtr_role}::
An operational identity within the
{at_recipe_bottle}
system
that an
{at_operator}
can assume to perform specific cloud operations.
Each
{rbtr_role}
is implemented through a dedicated
{giam_service_account}
and corresponding
{rbtgi_rbra_file}
containing the necessary credentials, with the exception of the
{rbtr_mason}
which operates exclusively within cloud infrastructure.

=== {giam_service_account} Definitions

[[rbtr_payor]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_payor}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that enables an
{at_operator}
to manage billing accounts and create depot projects using OAuth user authentication rather than service accounts.
The
{rbtr_payor}
operates through OAuth 2.0 desktop application flow, enabling individual developers to manage Recipe Bottle infrastructure without requiring organization-level permissions.

[[rbtr_governor]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_governor}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that grants an
{at_operator}
{gcp_project_id}
-scope Owner permissions without billing authority, designed for automation-friendly operations.

[[rbtr_retriever]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_retriever}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that allows an
{at_operator}
read-only access to the
{rbtgi_repository}
for pulling
{rbtgi_image_s}.

[[rbtr_mason]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_mason}::
A cloud-native
{giam_service_account}
that operates exclusively within the
{rbtgi_depot_project}
to execute
{at_recipe_bottle}
build instructions from the
{rbtr_director}.

The
{rbtr_mason}
impersonates the implicit service agent for builds, replacing Google's auto-created service agent with deterministic permissions and predictable authorization.

The
{rbtr_mason}
reads from the
{rbtgi_build_bucket},
carries out the build process within
{gcb_service},
and publishes
{rbtgi_image_s}
and
{rbtgi_metadata_s}
to the
{rbtgi_repository}
without requiring a local
{rbtgi_rbra_file}
since it operates entirely within the cloud infrastructure.

[[rbtr_director]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_director}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that grants an
{at_operator}
full build lifecycle permissions including Cloud Build submission and
{rbtgi_repository}
management.

=== Local Role Definitions

[[rbtr_marshal]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_marshal}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that qualifies and prepares releases for distribution.
Unlike other
{rbtr_role_s},
the
{rbtr_marshal}
has no
{giam_service_account}
or cloud identity — the
{rbtr_role}
taxonomy concerns operational authority, not authentication type.
Marshal operations are invisible to
{rbtr_consumer_s}.

=== {gcp_service} Definitions

[[rbtgi_depot_project]]
//axl_voices axig_project
{rbtgi_depot_project}::
The dedicated
{gcp_service}
project instance containing all Recipe Bottle depot resources, APIs, and
{giam_service_account_s}
for container building and management. Created and managed by the
{rbtr_payor}
role.

[[rbtgi_depot]]
{rbtgi_depot}::
A cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure comprising a
{rbtgi_depot_project},
{rbtgi_build_bucket},
{rbtgi_repository},
and
{rbtr_mason}
service account that share their productive lifecycle. Created as a unit by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}
and destroyed as a unit by
{rbtgo_depot_unmake}.

[[rbtgi_payor_project]]
//axl_voices axig_project
{rbtgi_payor_project}::
The administrative
{gcp_service}
project that hosts the
{rbtr_payor}
{giam_service_account}.
This
{gcp_project_id}
serves as the control plane for creating and managing multiple
{rbtgi_depot_project}
instances.


[[rbtgi_build_bucket]]
//axl_voices axig_bucket
{rbtgi_build_bucket}::
The dedicated
{gcs_bucket}
within the
{rbtgi_depot_project}
that holds
{at_recipe_bottle}
build artifacts with configured lifecycle policies.

=== {gar_service} Definitions

[[rbtgi_repository]]
//axl_voices axig_artifact_repository
{rbtgi_repository}::
The managed
{gar_service}
repository within the
{rbtgi_depot_project}
that stores
{at_recipe_bottle}
{rbtgi_image_s}
and metadata.

[[rbtgi_image]]
//axl_voices axig_container_image
{rbtgi_image}::
A
{at_recipe_bottle}
container image stored in the
{rbtgi_repository}
with associated tags and digest.

[[rbtgi_metadata]]
//axl_voices axig_generic_artifact
{rbtgi_metadata}::
A compressed archive containing
{at_recipe_bottle}
build transcript, SBOM, and provenance data stored in the
{rbtgi_repository}
as a Generic Artifact.
For multi-platform vessels, SBOMs and build information are per-platform — each architecture gets its own SBOM (generated by Syft scanning that platform's image) and its own `build_info.json`.
+
`build_info.json` fields are mode-aware — see {rbtga_ark_about} for the full field inventory.
Shared fields include `vessel_mode`, `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`; conjure adds SLSA provenance fields; bind adds `bind_source`; graft adds `graft_source`.

=== Ark Definitions

[[rbtga_ark]]
//axl_voices axig_artifact_group
{rbtga_ark}::
The coherent unit of a
{at_recipe_bottle}
build output: a set of artifacts in the
{rbtgi_repository}
sharing a common
{rbtga_hallmark}
but distinguished by suffix.
An ark comprises the
{rbtga_ark_image}
(`-image` suffix) containing the container image, the
{rbtga_ark_about}
(`-about` suffix) containing build metadata and SBOM, and the
{rbtga_ark_vouch}
(`-vouch` suffix) containing verification results.
+
An ark reaches the repository through one of three arrival paths:
+
* *Conjure*: A single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job builds the image (context delivered via pouch) and embeds about steps (via stitch), producing both `-image` and `-about`. Then {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verifies SLSA provenance via DSSE envelope signature verification in a separate Cloud Build job.
* *Bind*: A single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job copies the pinned upstream image and runs about steps, producing both `-image` and `-about`. Then {rbtgo_ark_vouch} records a digest-pin verdict in a separate Cloud Build job.
* *Graft*: Local docker/podman push places the image as `-image`. Then a single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job runs about steps followed by vouch steps, producing both `-about` and `-vouch` in one submission.
+
Conjure and bind each use two Cloud Build round-trips (primary job + vouch job). Graft uses one (combined about+vouch job), since the image arrives via local push rather than a Cloud Build step.
Vouch is mandatory: an ark without a vouch artifact is incomplete.
Arks are abjured atomically by
{rbtgo_ark_abjure}.

[[rbtga_hallmark]]
//axl_voices axtg_timestamp
{rbtga_hallmark}::
The dual-timestamp identifier marking when a vessel was hallmarked into an
{rbtga_ark}.
Format: `«MODE»YYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS` where `«MODE»` is a single character encoding the vessel mode.
+
Mode prefixes:
+
* `c` — conjure (Cloud Build build from Dockerfile)
* `b` — bind (mirror pinned upstream image)
* `g` — graft (local build pushed to GAR)
+
The `r` prefix on the second timestamp means "realized" — the moment the image materialized in GAR.
+
Example: `c260224153022-r260224160530` (27 characters).
Regex: `[cbg]\d{12}-r\d{12}`.
+
T1 (mode-prefixed): when the operation was initiated — inscribe for conjure, mirror for bind, OCI image creation time for graft.
T2 (r-prefixed): when the image landed in GAR.
+
For graft, T1 is an intrinsic fact about the image (its `created` metadata), not an operational timestamp.
Reproducible builds may produce `T1=700101000000` — this is a true statement, not an error.
+
Combined with vessel to form complete artifact tags.
All persistent artifact tags use the full hallmark:
per-platform image tags as `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-image-amd64`, `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-image-arm64`;
consumer-facing manifest list as `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-image`;
about as `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-about`;
vouch as `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-vouch`.
Platform suffix is derived from the OCI platform (e.g., `linux/amd64` -> `-amd64`, `linux/arm64` -> `-arm64`, `linux/arm/v7` -> `-armv7`).
Multiple conjures of the same inscription produce distinct hallmarks (unique realized timestamps) with independently-attested images.
+
For conjure, SLSA provenance is generated via the {rbtgr_build_json} `images:` field, which lists inscribe-time-predictable alias tags (`«VESSEL»:«INSCRIBE_TS»-image-«SUFFIX»`).
These alias tags exist in the local Docker daemon (created during pullback) so that {gcb_service} can push and attest them.
Provenance is bound to image digests, not tags — the hallmark-tagged copies share the same digests and inherit the attestations.
Alias tags are ephemeral; re-conjure overwrites them in the registry. Hallmark-tagged artifacts persist.

[[rbtga_ark_image]]
//axl_voices axig_container_image
{rbtga_ark_image}::
The container image artifact within an
{rbtga_ark},
tagged with the `-image` suffix.
Contains the executable container layers, regardless of how the image arrived in GAR.
+
* *Conjure*: built by {gcb_service} from a Dockerfile. SLSA v1.0 Level 3 provenance is auto-generated.
* *Bind*: mirrored from a pinned upstream registry reference. No SLSA provenance (upstream image, not locally built).
* *Graft*: locally built and pushed via native docker/podman push. No SLSA provenance (no Cloud Build involvement).
+
**Single-arch vessels**: a single-platform image. For conjure, pushed via the `images:` field with SLSA provenance.
+
**Multi-platform vessels** (conjure only): the consumer-facing `-image` tag is a multi-platform manifest list assembled by `imagetools create` from per-platform tags (e.g., `-image-amd64`, `-image-arm64`, `-image-armv7`).
Per-platform tags are internal build plumbing with independent SLSA v1.0 Level 3 provenance sharing a common `buildInvocationId`.
The combined manifest list has `slsa_build_level: unknown` (expected — Cloud Build did not build the manifest list); consumers pulling the combined tag get transparent platform resolution to attested per-platform images.
Bind vessels may be multi-platform (upstream images often are; crane copy preserves the manifest list).
Graft vessels are typically single-platform.

[[rbtga_ark_about]]
//axl_voices axig_generic_artifact
{rbtga_ark_about}::
The metadata artifact within an
{rbtga_ark},
tagged with the `-about` suffix.
Contains SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generated by Syft and `build_info.json` assembled by the
{rbtgo_ark_about}
Cloud Build steps.
Created uniformly across all three vessel modes as part of a Cloud Build job: conjure and bind embed about steps in their primary image job; graft combines about and vouch steps in a single metadata job.
When a Dockerfile path is configured in the vessel regime, its content is embedded as `recipe.txt` (subject to ~4KB Cloud Build substitution variable size limit; omitted with a warning if exceeded).
+
`build_info.json` is a nested JSON object with mode-aware fields:
root — `hallmark`, `vessel_mode` (`conjure`/`bind`/`graft`), `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`, `about_timestamp`;
`git` object — `repo`, `branch`, `commit`;
conjure `build` object — `build_id`, `inscribe_timestamp`, `qemu_used`;
conjure `slsa` object — `build_level`, `build_invocation_id`, `provenance_predicate_types`, `provenance_builder_id`;
bind `bind` object — `source` (original upstream image reference);
graft `graft` object — `source` (local image reference at push time).
+
**Single-arch vessels**: a single-platform `-about` container with one SBOM and one `build_info.json`.
+
**Multi-platform vessels**: built with `buildx --push` using `FROM scratch` and Docker's automatic `TARGETARCH`/`TARGETVARIANT` build arguments to select per-platform files.
Each platform's layer contains that architecture's SBOM and `build_info.json`.
The `-about` container mirrors the `-image` container's platform list.

[[rbtga_ark_vouch]]
//axl_voices axig_verification_artifact
{rbtga_ark_vouch}::
The mandatory verification artifact within an
{rbtga_ark},
tagged with the `-vouch` suffix.
Created by
{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
as a
{gcb_service}
job submitted after
{rbtgo_ark_about}
completes.
The vouch verdict depends on vessel mode:
+
* *Conjure*: DSSE envelope signature verification of GCB-signed provenance against each per-platform digest. Records per-platform verification verdicts.
* *Bind*: Digest-pin comparison — verifies the image digest in GAR matches the pinned upstream reference. No SLSA provenance exists for upstream images.
* *Graft*: GRAFTED stamp — records that the image was locally built and pushed without provenance chain. An explicit trust boundary marker.
+
The vouch artifact is a multi-platform `FROM scratch` container holding `vouch_summary.json` and per-platform `verify-«SUFFIX».json` files (conjure) or a single `vouch_summary.json` (bind/graft).
Content is architecture-independent metadata; multi-platform is for pull ergonomics (no platform mismatch warning on arm64 hosts).
+
**Vouch depends on about**: {rbtgo_ark_vouch} gates on `-about` existence before submission.
The about pipeline must complete before vouch can run.
+
**DSSE envelope verification** (conjure mode only, ₣Av):
Provenance signatures verified against embedded GCB attestor public keys from `projects/verified-builder` KMS.
Step 1 (Alpine) writes the attestor public key to `/workspace/keys/`.
Step 2 (gcloud/Python 3) fetches provenance via `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance`, extracts the v1.0 DSSE envelope, decodes base64url payload + signature, reconstructs PAE, and verifies via `openssl dgst -sha256 -verify` subprocess against the embedded attestor key.
No slsa-verifier, no jq — Python `json` + `base64` modules + openssl subprocess only.
+
**Vouch Cloud Build job architecture**:
Vouch uses the RunBuild API (`builds.create`) rather than trigger dispatch — the hallmark is known only after the image arrives in GAR, so a pre-registered trigger cannot provide it.
The build runs as the {rbtr_mason} service account.
The vouch step branches on `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`: conjure runs DSSE provenance verification, bind runs digest-pin comparison, graft stamps GRAFTED.
+
Discovered constraints from implementation:
+
  * **Private worker pool**: The RunBuild API caller ({rbtr_director}) requires `cloudbuild.workerpools.use` to specify a private pool, a permission not included in `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor`.
    {rbtgo_director_knight} grants `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` at project scope.
    Vouch runs on the same private pool as conjure (`RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM`), eliminating an untested default-pool-with-buildx permutation.
  * **gcloud exception**: Vouch verification (conjure mode) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe` for provenance retrieval (see {rbsk_no_gcloud} exception).
    All other steps use the GCE metadata server for OAuth tokens and direct registry REST API calls.
  * **Builder image selection**: Step 1 (Alpine) writes attestor keys (conjure) or is a no-op (bind/graft).
    Step 2 (gcloud/Python 3) runs mode-aware verification.
    Step 3 (Docker) assembles and pushes the vouch container (`docker buildx build --push` for multi-platform).
  * **GCB dollar escaping**: Step scripts embedded in Build JSON require `$$` for literal `$` and un-escaped `${_RBGV_*}` for GCB substitution variables, following the same `sed` pattern as conjure's `${_RBGY_*}`.
  * **DSSE verification**: Provenance envelope signatures verified against embedded GCB attestor public keys (`projects/verified-builder` KMS). PAE reconstruction + `openssl dgst -sha256 -verify` subprocess. No slsa-verifier, no jq — Python 3 standard library only.
    builds.create provenance achieves SLSA Build Level 3 (₢AvAAB research confirmed).
+
**Idempotent re-vouch**:
Re-running {rbtgo_ark_vouch} on a hallmark that already has a `-vouch` artifact re-executes verification.
For conjure and bind, the new vouch content should match the existing vouch (same digests + same provenance/pin + same verifier = identical output).
For graft, the GRAFTED stamp is always identical.
A mismatch for conjure/bind indicates provenance change or tampering and must produce a loud alarm.
If determinism proves unreliable in practice, narrow the comparison to verification verdict, digests, and builder identity.
+
**Abjure**: {rbtgo_ark_abjure} checks for and deletes the `-vouch` artifact alongside `-image` and `-about`.
+
**Display**: {rbtgo_hallmark_tally} includes a Vouch column showing `yes`/`no` per hallmark, detected by matching the `-vouch` tag suffix in the GAR tag list.

[[rbtga_vessel]]
//axl_voices axig_build_specification
{rbtga_vessel}::
A named container image specification in the regime that defines what to build and how it arrives in the
{rbtgi_repository}.
Each vessel has a
{rbrv_vessel_mode}
(conjure, bind, or graft) that determines its arrival path, and produces
{rbtga_ark_s}
stamped with a
{rbtga_hallmark}
on each successful build.
+
A vessel's regime file (`rbrv.env`) declares its mode, Dockerfile path (conjure), upstream reference (bind), or local image (graft), along with platform targets and build configuration.
The vessel name appears in all artifact tags as the stem before the hallmark.

=== Rubric Definitions

[[rbtgr_rubric]]
//axl_voices axig_build_config
{rbtgr_rubric}::
The per-vessel build definition comprising a generated
{rbtgr_build_json}
and build context delivered via pouch (FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR).
Builds submitted directly via `builds.create` API.

[[rbtgr_build_json]]
//axl_voices axig_build_config
{rbtgr_build_json}::
The generated `cloudbuild.json` for a vessel, produced from step scripts and regime pins by the stitch function.
All values resolved directly at stitch time — no placeholders, no post-processing.
Submitted via `builds.create` API. Build context delivered via pouch (FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR).
See <<rbscj_trade_study>>.

[[rbtgr_provenance]]
//axl_voices axig_slsa_provenance
{rbtgr_provenance}::
SLSA v1.0 build provenance attestation auto-generated by Google for `builds.create` submissions.
Records build inputs, environment, and outputs as a cryptographically signed attestation on the built image.
+
Requires two conditions in the
{rbtgr_build_json}:
(1) `options.requestedVerifyOption: VERIFIED` — opts private pool builds into provenance generation (private pools default to no provenance);
(2) the top-level `images:` field must list the image URI — Cloud Build generates provenance only for images it pushes via this field.
Cloud Build pre-populates Docker credentials for all GAR regions, so explicit docker-login steps are unnecessary.
+
**Single-arch vessels**: `buildx --load` places the built image into the local Docker daemon's store.
The `images:` field in {rbtgr_build_json} causes Cloud Build to push the image and generate SLSA v1.0 Level 3 provenance.
Verified on builds.create submissions — rbev-busybox on depot10030 (3 platforms: amd64, arm64, armv7) with DSSE envelope signature verification (₣Av ₢AvAAD).
+
**Single-arch pipeline** (3 steps + `images:` push, single build invocation):
+
  1. `derive-tag-base` — compute {rbtga_hallmark} from inscribe + realized timestamps; writes to `/builder/outputs/output` for strong-tie discovery via `results.buildStepOutputs[0]`
  2. `qemu-binfmt` — register cross-architecture emulation (skipped if native platform)
  3. `build-and-load` — `buildx --load` into local Docker daemon (single platform)
+
About assembly (syft, build_info, metadata push) uses reusable {rbtgo_ark_about} scripts (`rbgja/`) embedded in the stitched JSON alongside the build steps.
+
**Multi-platform vessels**: production-validated architecture.
Docker's local store (classic image store on CB workers, Docker 20.10.24) is single-platform only — `buildx --load` cannot load multi-platform images.
The validated approach uses per-platform pullback:
`buildx --push` builds all platforms and pushes a multi-platform manifest to GAR;
`docker pull --platform <arch>` pulls each platform variant back to the local daemon (requires Experimental: true, confirmed on CB workers);
each pulled image is tagged with a per-platform tag (e.g., `:TAG-amd64`, `:TAG-arm64`);
the `images:` field declares all per-platform tags — Cloud Build pushes each and generates independent SLSA v1.0 Level 3 provenance with the same `buildInvocationId`, proving single-build origin;
`docker buildx imagetools create` then reassembles a multi-platform manifest list from the per-platform tags.
+
Validated in experiments and production on depot `demo1025`:
per-platform provenance (`b3fd60c7`) — all 3 platforms (amd64, arm64, armv7) achieved SLSA Level 3 with shared `buildInvocationId`;
manifest reassembly (`8cd7b713`) — `imagetools create` produced a combined manifest list, per-platform provenance preserved;
production e2e (`2e172ce0`) — trigger-dispatched build of `rbev-busybox` (3 platforms) achieved SLSA Level 3 on all per-platform images with shared `buildInvocationId`, consumer-facing manifest list resolved correctly.
The combined manifest list itself has `slsa_build_level: unknown` (expected — Cloud Build did not build the manifest list artifact), but consumers pulling the combined tag get transparent platform resolution to attested per-platform images with full SLSA Level 3.
+
Implementation notes:
`docker pull --platform` works on CB workers (Docker 20.10.24, Experimental: true);
`$${VAR}` escaping required for shell variables in {rbtgr_build_json} (Cloud Build interprets `${VAR}` as substitution variables);
`jq` is not available in `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` — the `--platform` flag approach avoids JSON manifest parsing entirely.
See `Memos/memo-20260305-provenance-architecture-gap.md` for full experiment details.
+
**Target multi-platform pipeline** (6 steps + `images:` push, single build invocation — validated by experiment 6, build `6661d0cd`):
+
  1. `derive-tag-base` — compute {rbtga_hallmark} from inscribe + realized timestamps
  2. `qemu-binfmt` — register cross-architecture emulation
  3. `buildx --push` — build all platforms, push intermediate `-multi` tag to GAR
  4. `per-platform-pullback` — `docker pull --platform <arch>` for each platform, `docker tag` to hallmark-tagged and inscribe-time alias tags
  5. `docker push` — pre-push each hallmark-tagged per-platform image to registry (enables in-build `imagetools create`)
  6. `imagetools create` — assemble consumer-facing multi-platform manifest list from per-platform tags (in-build step, not post-build)
+
About assembly (syft, build_info, metadata push) uses reusable {rbtgo_ark_about} scripts (`rbgja/`) embedded in the stitched JSON alongside the build steps.
+
`images:` lists all per-platform tags — Cloud Build re-pushes each (idempotent, same digest) and generates SLSA provenance.
`options.requestedVerifyOption: VERIFIED` ensures build fails if provenance cannot be generated.
Per-platform tags are internal build plumbing; the consumer-facing tag (assembled by `imagetools create`) is platform-transparent, identical to single-arch.
